sections.title: Preface
paragraphs: Dynamic Circuit Strategies for Long-Range Entanglement under Local Connectivity
Static Chains, Entanglement Swapping, and PRX Quantum 5.030339 Reproduction
Reproducible simulation study (Qiskit Aer) — integrated graduate project report
HE, CHIEN-YI Prof. Hsiu-Chuan Hsu Department of Applied Physics / Electronic Physics Program, National Chengchi University June 2026

sections.title: Abstract
paragraphs: We study long-range Bell-state preparation between endpoint qubits on a ladder connectivity graph where two-qubit gates are restricted to nearest-neighbor nodes (Ephys Challenge 2026). Strategy A (static GHZ chain, 2L−1 CNOTs) is compared with Strategy B (entanglement swapping, L CNOTs, odd L) under one shared Qiskit Aer noise model (8192 shots). After reading Bäumer et al. (PRX Quantum 5, 030339), we explicitly separate (i) competition-original ladder simulations, (ii) circuit reproduction of Fig. 1(a) and Fig. 2(b), and (iii) qualitative crossover / error-budget analysis mapped to Section III and Appendix B.2. Table 1 lists every paper location and whether we imitate its mechanism or reproduce its circuits.
Keywords: dynamic quantum circuits, long-range entanglement, entanglement swapping, ladder topology, NISQ

sections.title: Author contributions
sections.tagline: Ephys Challenge ladder circuits, resource formulas, and noisy crossover (original competition work).
paragraphs: Independent reproduction of PRX Quantum Fig. 1(a) LRCX and Fig. 2(b) GHZ circuits (35 pytest checks).
Paper-mapped error-budget analysis (Appendix B.2) and qualitative crossover discussion (Section III).

sections.title: 1. Introduction
paragraphs: ▌ PRX qualitative analogy — Introduction — dynamic circuits under local connectivity. Our ladder endpoints e₀,e₁ problem is competition-specific; we use the paper as the benchmark for measurement-based routing and for quantitative circuit reproduction.

Superconducting processors restrict two-qubit gates to nearest neighbors. Dynamic circuits use mid-circuit measurement and feed-forward for effectively constant-depth routing [Bäumer et al., PRX Quantum 5, 030339 (2024)]. On a ladder graph we ask: under fair noise, how do static GHZ-style Bell preparation and measurement-based entanglement swapping trade CNOT resources against endpoint fidelity?

sections.title: 2. Bell states and verification
paragraphs: Target |Φ+⟩ on endpoints (e₀,e₁) ↔ (q₀,q_L); P(00)+P(11) is the primary noisy diagnostic.
Ideal checks: statevector partial trace, CHSH correlators (Tsirelson bound 2√2).
Swapping uses Bell measurement + X/Z feed-forward (same Pauli-frame logic as PRX LRCX).
Static chain: H(e₀) → forward CNOT → backward CNOT uncomputes interior qubits.
Figure 1. Ladder topology and endpoint notation (competition setting).
Figure 2. Strategy A: single-rail static chain abstraction.
Figure 3. Zigzag Hamiltonian path on the ladder (reference topology).

sections.title: 3.1 Circuit families (Ephys Challenge)
paragraphs: ▌ PRX mechanism (imitation) — Bell measurement + Pauli feed-forward throughout Fig. 1(a). Strategy B (entanglement swapping) implements the same teleportation-style frame corrections as the dynamic LRCX branch, adapted to ladder Bell preparation rather than gate teleportation.

Strategy A: H(e₀) → forward CX → reverse CX (2L−1 CNOTs). Strategy B: adjacent |Φ+⟩ pairs, Bell measurement, X/Z feed-forward (L CNOTs, odd L). Zigzag uses 2(L+1) qubits on full ladder edges (topology reference). Noisy runs use classical post-decode when if_test is unstable in Aer.

sections.title: 3.2 Shared noise model (Ephys simulations)
paragraphs: Table 2. Shared Aer noise parameters (not PRX device calibration).

sections.title: 3.3 PRX Quantum 5.030339: imitation vs. reproduction map
paragraphs: The following table is the central reference for reviewers: each row cites a specific location in PRX Quantum 5, 030339 and states whether our work reproduces the published circuit/analysis or only imitates the underlying mechanism / draws a qualitative analogy. Reproduce = rebuilt circuits or formulas with automated tests; Imitate = same physics idea in a different task; Qualitative analogy = compare trends only (different noise calibration).
Table 1. Mapping to Bäumer et al., PRX Quantum 5, 030339 (2024): what we imitate vs. reproduce.

sections.title: 3.4 PRX circuit reproduction (code)
paragraphs: ▌ PRX reproduce — Fig. 1(a) and Fig. 2(b) — full circuit families. 35 pytest assertions on statevector fidelity and dynamic/unitary equivalence; see Table 4–5.
Listing 1. PRX equivalence tests.
cd prx
python3 -m pytest verify/test_equivalence.py -q # 35 passed

sections.title: 4.1 Gate resources
paragraphs: ▌ PRX qualitative analogy — Fig. 2(c) — CNOT / depth scaling. Our ideal CNOT vs L plot mirrors the paper's resource comparison spirit; Strategy A aligns with unitary GHZ scaling (2L−1), swapping with lower CX for odd L.
Figure 4. Ideal CNOT count vs L.
Table 3. Resources at L=5 (from resource_formula_table.csv).

sections.title: 4.2 CHSH verification
paragraphs: ▌ Ephys Challenge (original) — —. Ideal CHSH sanity check for |Φ+⟩ endpoints (Ephys competition analysis).
Figure 5. CHSH correlator: ideal (flat at 2√2) vs noisy static/zigzag.

sections.title: 4.3 Noisy Bell diagnostic and crossover
paragraphs: ▌ PRX qualitative analogy — Section III — dynamic vs. unitary crossover on hardware. Static (unitary-depth) vs. swapping (measurement-heavy) under Aer noise parallels the paper's short-distance unitary advantage and long-distance dynamic trend; parameters differ — trends only.
Table 4. P(00)+P(11) with 95% Wilson CI (8192 shots).
Figure 6. Noisy crossover with 95% CI (static, zigzag, swapping).
Primary comparison: static vs swapping on single-rail endpoints. Zigzag (two-rail) uses more qubits/CNOT but respects full ladder edges; included as topology reference, not primary claim.

sections.title: 4.4 PRX circuit reproduction (quantitative)
paragraphs: ▌ PRX reproduce — Fig. 1(a) — LRCX dynamic / unitary / postprocessing. Table 5: minimum Bell fidelity over measurement branches (statevector simulation).
Table 5. LRCX Bell fidelity (statevector, min over measurement branches) — PRX Fig. 1(a).
▌ PRX reproduce — Fig. 2(b) — unitary vs dynamic GHZ preparation. Table 6: GHZ stabilizer fidelity from rebuilt circuits.
Table 6. GHZ preparation fidelity — PRX Fig. 2(b).

sections.title: 4.5 Error-budget analysis (PRX Appendix B.2)
paragraphs: ▌ PRX reproduce — Appendix B.2 — λ_tot = t_idle λ_idle + N_CNOT λ_CNOT + N_meas λ_meas. We apply the paper's μ≈3.65, λ_idle=0.03, λ_CNOT=0.02, λ_meas=0.03 separately to Bell prep and LRCX tasks. These bounds use paper calibration — not our Aer model. Qualitative trend mapping only.
Figure 7. Error budget: Bell-state preparation tasks (Appendix B.2 mapping).
Figure 8. Error budget: long-range CNOT teleportation (Appendix B.2 mapping).

sections.title: 4.6 Representative circuits
paragraphs: ▌ PRX qualitative analogy — Fig. 2(b) left — unitary GHZ chain. Strategy A static chain at L=5.
Figure 9. Static chain (L=5).
▌ PRX mechanism (imitation) — Fig. 1(a) — Bell meas. + feed-forward. Strategy B swapping at L=3.
Figure 10. Entanglement swapping (L=3).
Listing 2. Full reproduction.
git clone https://github.com/real-louis/Dynamic-Circuit-Strategies-for-Long-Range-Entanglement-under-Local-Connectivity
cd Dynamic-Circuit-Strategies-for-Long-Range-Entanglement-under-Local-Connectivity
pip install -r requirements.txt
python3 scripts/reproduce.py

sections.title: 5. Discussion and limitations
paragraphs: Ephys ladder Bell preparation is our original competition scope; PRX sections are reproduced or mapped as in Table 1.
Simulation uses Qiskit Aer; PRX Quantum reports 101-qubit IBM hardware — do not equate absolute fidelities.
Error-budget curves (Appendix B.2) and P(00)+P(11) (Aer) use different calibrations — compare trends only.
Bell-state preparation ≠ LRCX gate teleportation; related via Bell measurement but distinct tasks.
Classical decode approximates feed-forward for fair noisy Ephys comparison.

sections.title: 6. Conclusion
paragraphs: We deliver a competition-format ladder Bell report extended with an explicit PRX Quantum 5.030339 map: circuits in Fig. 1(a) and Fig. 2(b) are reproduced with pytest; Appendix B.2 error budgets and Section III crossover phenomena are mapped qualitatively to our Ephys simulations.
